title: Jacquemontia densiflora
taxonomy: Jacquemontia densiflora is classified within the family Convolvulaceae. This family includes over 1,800 species of flowering plants, commonly known as morning glories and bindweeds. Jacquemontia densiflora is a climbing plant that belongs to the genus Jacquemontia, which consists of approximately 50 species. The genus Jacquemontia is native to tropical regions of Asia and the Americas. Jacquemontia densiflora is further classified within the order Solanales.

intro: Jacquemontia densiflora, also known as the Jamaican nightshade or night-scented jasmine, is a tropical vine native to the Caribbean and Central America. This plant is valued for its various health benefits, including its antidiarrheal, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat digestive issues and infections.
